personalNote:
Notion is the one piece of software that has shaped how I think about knowledge work more than any other. I've been a power user since 2020, and it's become the connective tissue of every team I've worked on since: our sales pipeline at Smarter Technologies lived in Notion, our project tracking at Thoughtful ran through Notion, and my own career infrastructure - including the job hunt system that produced these very applications - is built on top of Notion's API.

What draws me to Notion now is the same thing that drew me to it then: the belief that the best productivity tool is the one that disappears and lets you do the work. The AI features Notion is shipping are the first time I've seen a major productivity platform get that balance right. The co-pilot metaphor is tired everywhere else, but in Notion it actually fits - it augments the structure you've already built rather than replacing it with a black box.

I built the Notion Meeting Intelligence system because I wanted to prove that point to myself. Our sales team was losing context between meetings, deals were slipping because follow-up was inconsistent. I built a multi-agent pipeline that took our Zoom recordings and turned them into structured deal intelligence written directly into our Notion workspace. It ran live on $31.8M in pipeline. That experience, building AI-native workflows that live inside Notion rather than alongside it, is exactly what I want to keep doing here.
